steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content: The strongest signal is “once I had sent off the manuscript”—the narrator expects to dispatch her completed work and return to normal life. This action requires completing to mean finishing.

steps.title: Match the word to its meaning
steps.content: ”Completing” means finishing the work. The narrator will send off the manuscript after it's done, so completing clearly expresses the act of bringing the translation to an end. Destroying, advertising, and rejecting contradict the progression toward dispatch. That matches choice B — B is correct.
